items.headline: In his first Game 7, Jarrett Allen delivers a 22-point, 19-rebound night to help Cavs advance
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — Jarrett Allen is usually laid back before games, including interacting with the crowd during the last minute of pregame warmups.
That wasn't the case Sunday night as he played in his first Game 7.

items.headline: Injuries and a brutal third quarter end the Raptors’ playoff return in Game 7 loss
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — Scottie Barnes and RJ Barrett did everything they could to try to get the Toronto Raptors into the Eastern Conference semifinals.
However, a couple of key injuries and a more veteran group ended the Raptors' first playoff trip in four years.

items.headline: Cale Makar returns from injury, scores twice as Avalanche outlast Wild 9-6 in wacky Game 1
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — A tight game was expected. A game featuring 15 goals between two of the top defensive teams?
“If you scripted that one," Colorado coach Jared Bednar said, “I don’t know how you do. I can't explain it."

items.headline: Pistons advance in playoffs for 1st time in 18 years, beating Magic 116-94
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Cade Cunningham had 32 points and 12 assists, Tobias Harris added 30 points and the Detroit Pistons beat the Orlando Magic 116-94 in Game 7 on Sunday to win a playoff series for the first time in 18 years.
Cunningham averaged 32.4 points for Detroit, which last won a postseason series by beating Orlando in the second round in 2008. The Pistons advance to play the winner of Sunday evening’s Game 7 between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Toronto Raptors. Game 1 will be Tuesday at Little Caesars Arena.

items.headline: Allen ties playoff career high with 22 points as Cavaliers beat Raptors 114-102 to advance
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — Jarrett Allen tied his playoff career high with 22 points and grabbed 19 rebounds as the Cleveland Cavaliers advanced to the Eastern Conference semifinals with a 114-102 victory over the Toronto Raptors in Game 7 of their series Sunday night.
Donovan Mitchell led the Cavaliers with 22 points and James Harden added 18 in a series in which the home team won all seven games.

items.headline: 4th straight 1st-round exit leaves the Lightning searching for answers
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — The Tampa Bay Lightning dominated most of Game 7, had triple the scoring opportunities and still found a way to lose.
It’s an early exit for the fourth straight season.

items.headline: Wrobleski throws 6 scoreless innings as Dodgers snap 4-game slide with victory over Cardinals
items.description:
ST. LOUIS (AP) — Justin Wrobleski tossed six scoreless innings to help the Los Angeles Dodgers beat the St. Louis Cardinals 4-1 on Sunday to snap a four-game losing streak.
Wrobleski (5-0) allowed six hits and a walk. Tanner Scott pitched a scoreless ninth for his second save of the season.

items.headline: Torkelson's home run, McGonigle's RBI singles lift Tigers past Rangers, 7-1
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Spencer Torkelson hit a two-run homer and the Detroit Tigers pulled away to a 7-1 victory over the Texas Rangers on Sunday night.
Torkelson had gone five games without a homer after tying a franchise record by homering in five consecutive games.

items.headline: Canadiens outlast Lightning 2-1 in Game 7 to win thrilling 1st-round series
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Alex Newhook broke a tie with 8:53 left and the Montreal Canadiens outlasted the Tampa Bay Lightning 2-1 on Game 7 on Sunday night to end the thrilling first-round series.
The Canadiens will face the Buffalo Sabres in the second round after finishing off their first series victory since losing the Stanley Cup Final to Tampa Bay in 2021. Game 1 is Wednesday night in Buffalo.

items.headline: Anthony Volpe's rehab ends and Yankees option shortstop to Triple-A with José Caballero playing well
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Volpe’s rehab from left shoulder surgery ended Sunday, but the shortstop’s next step is not rejoining the New York Yankees.
With José Caballero excelling as New York's everyday shortstop, the AL East-leading Yankees announced they had optioned Volpe to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.
